I purchased the portfolio case at the same time I got my Xoom from Verizon , so I didn ' t have the benefit of seeing the reviews here . As has been said by many others , the lack of an opening for the charging and usb ports is disappointing . That , combined with the rather high price cost it a couple stars . As a protective cover , I think it does quite well . It ' s sturdy and the materials from which it ' s made should protect the Xoom if it ' s bumped around and , to some extent , from the elements . It holds the Xoom securely . I ' ve no fear that it ' s going to just slip off or anything . I ' m not too keen on the way it clips over the top of the Xoom when closed . It seems that there should be a better way to hold it shut without having to pry with one ' s fingernails or fingertips to open it again . It also lacks any way to keep it open if you swing the cover all the way around as you would if you were reading an ebook or just laying the Xoom flat in your lap . Overall , I like the portfolio case , though I think I ' m likely to find another case and use the portfolio case for a bit of extra protection when I ' m traveling . I figure it ' ll be great for some extra protection when I ' m on a long flight and won ' t need access to the charging port til I ' m at my destination .

This speaker has perfect connectivity with my Macbook Pro when I use the USB adapter ( included ) - - no cutouts whatsoever . It can also connect without the USB adapter using the Macbook ' s built - in bluetooth , but for me the USB is simpler and more reliable . ( Satechi BT connectivity was terrible with my Macbook ) . After the initial setup ( quick and easy ) , the Macbook remembers the speaker and connects instantly and seamlessly . Sound quality is excellent , considering the size & price , and better than the Satechi . Love the portability and the rechargeable battery - - this speaker is truly 100 % wireless . There are only 2 things lacking from this speaker : a remote control ( you can ' t control the volume through your computer , so you have to physically adjust the controls on the back of the speaker ) , and an output jack , which would allow you to play computer audio through your home stereo speakers from across the room ( the Satechi does have this ) . Nonetheless it ' s the best wireless speaker I have found for the price and I am very happy .

I bought this camera looking for something rather simple to take pictures of service projects sponsored by my non - profit group , Service for Peace . I wanted a camera that was not too expensive , rather light and easy to carry , that would take XD cards ( since I already had some from a previous camera ) , and that would be easy to use , since I often ask other people to take pictures at our events . Naturally , I also wanted clear shots , a durable camera , long battery life , and all those things . Another requirement was that the camera NOT have a zoom lens . All things being equal , a good fixed lens takes better pictures than a zoom lens , and I don ' t mind moving around to find the best shot . I ' ve also noticed that zoom lenses on digital cameras , because they have to stick out , can be easily dirtied or damaged . Fiddling around with the zoom control seems to make it easy to miss quick candid shots that look really good for documenting projects like ours . With this camera ' s 5 megapixel resolution , there is no problem cropping down to just the exact picture I need , and since I ' m doing it at home with editing software , I can take my time and get it just right . I can also use the same shot for both close - ups and far - away shots . So how did this camera shape up ? It is light and easy to carry , and it is simple to operate . It generally works well for documenting my projects . I do have a few beefs with it , however . The most important is that I have been having a problem from time - to - time recently with the camera saying the door for the memory card is not closed , even though it is . This is a problem because the camera will not do anything else until it is satisfied the door is closed . So far , I have been able to get the camera working by opening and closing the door several times , and reseating the memory card , but I know the day will eventually come when I will miss an important shot because of this problem . Also , I fear they day will come soon when just opening and closing the door will no longer solve the problem . Another issue I have found is that if you put the camera in your pocket , it is quite easy to slide the OFF / ON button to the ON position . This does wear out the battery a bit before the camera goes into sleep mode , but a greater problem is that the whole time the camera is in your pocket , the lens is open , and can easily be damaged . A more recessed OFF / ON switch could solve this problem . There is a considerable delay from the time when you press the shutter button until the shot is taken . I know this is a common problem in digital cameras , as the camera focuses , determines the exposure , and the like , but I find it annoying , and it has cost me many excellent candid and action shots . By the time the shutter actually operates , the moment is long gone . I was raised on 35 mm range finder and SLR cameras , and I really value instantaneous response when I take a picture . The last issue I have with the camera is that when pictures are blown up , they are not very clear , even if taken at infinity . The lens is not sharp at all . Previously , I owned a cheaper camera , a Fuji Finepix A200 , and the pictures were sharp as far as you could blow them up , but this is not the case with this camera . Another beef I have is that the individual pixels often are colors that jar significantly with the colors of the area they are representing , such as green or blue pixels in the area of a person ' s face . I assume this is an artifact of the compression algorithm used by the camera , but it looks pretty bad if you need to blow up a section of a picture in a situation , such as on a computer screen , where individual pixels are visible . Still , I do find this camera useful , and although I have been tempted to take it back , I ' m still using it . I give it three out of five stars .

I think the easiest way to review the Targus Brilliance II Backpack for 16 " Laptops is to literally start from the back and work my way out . The first thing I noticed about this backpack is the very comfortable padding on the pack while it rests on your back . The padding is thick but not annoying , and is feel quite nice on my back . The straps are also padded quite well . They include a smaller than average cellphone holder , which is my biggest gripe about this pack . The first chamber in the pack holds laptops up to sixteen inches . My laptop , a Dell Latitude E6400 fits quite snugly in the compartment . In this same chamber is a second , less padded area for holding short cables , power supplies , or any other tools of the trade that you don ' t mind being loose in a bag . There is also a compartment for holding devices such as an Ipod or MP3 player and an access point for earphones as well that can be found on the top of the bag under the carrying handle . The location of the bag is right over the velcro strap that holds your the laptop compartment closed , so you have to lift this little bag to get to your laptop every time unless you tuck the compartment and the strap behind your laptop and do not use it . This is just a minor aggravation . The second chamber opens wide and offers even more space for loose equipment . There are no pockets in this chamber . The third , and smallest , chamber features a key ring suitable for keys or even flash drives to hang from . There is also a large pocket with one smaller enclosed pocket and one mesh pocket on it , as well as pencil pockets . Near the aforementioned padded handle is another loop to hang more things off of . Two rings suitable for sticking your thumbs in while you walk are available on the straps . These make for easy adjustments of the straps to fit your body better . Overall , this backpack is very , very comfortable to tote around . With all of the padding , it doesn ' t force you to stand at attention the entire time while carrying around a hard laptop . Only time will tell how durable this backpack is , but with Targus ' reputation , I expect it to last for a long time . Recommended .

I am a bit of an audiophile and I bought this knowing full well that it was not commiserate with a high end 7 . 1 system or even the Zvoxx system which this is a blatant copy of . Zvoxx came first having invented this first and sonically it ' s a superior system . But here is where Bose has made it ' s fortunes : by allowing you to pay off whatever you buy in monthly installments on their website . Brilliant ! Suddenly you have enhanced sound in your life for a mere $ 30 - 40 a month ! That ' s how they manage to sell their products without anyone hearing them . So to buy it here seems silly to me . And if you have the $ 400 go for the Zvoxx because it is just deeper , bassier and better all around . BUT if you have limited funds and want your cookie NOW , then head on over to the website and make a deal with their devil and go for it . What you will get : a perfectly fine system for your bedroom or office . It is highly competent . No big bass but enough when it ' s cranked up . I got mine for my bedroom and it ' s great . Sure I wish that it had a headphone connection . That would be nice . And remember : use optical cord provided ONLY . As they themselves note : it is the vastly superior connection ( light impulses instead of hard wire ) . Makes a big difference . Perfection ? Hardly ? Sturdy and dependable with great service ? Absolutely . I have their noise cancelling headphones and the wave radio at my bedside . Both fantastic .

Do not purchase this camera ! This camera was purchased for a family trip to Maui and I was very excited about using it for snorkeling . The camera ( black olympus 1030sw ) had worked great for my father the previous year and as such 4 more cameras were purchased by friends / family ( green olympus 1030sw ) . Within 5 days all 4 green cameras had stopped working due to water entering the supposedly water tight battery or USB compartments . I was successfully able to use my new camera for 1 day , but upon entering the water on day 2 the camera ' s LCD screen stopped working . Of the other 3 other broken cameras , 1 stopped working immediately after going underwater , 1 stopped working on the 3rd snorkel trip and the other on snorkel day 5 . While in Maui I did a quick internet search to find that this is a very common issue [ . . . ] with the Olympus 1030sw . Additionally , while on a snorkel trip to Lanai one of the deckhands on the boat overheard our conversation about the camera and indicated hers died after 10 underwater trips . She also said this has happened to many people / customers taking her snorkel tour . The 1 functional unit in our group ( black camera ) was perfectly fine for a previous 2 week Hawaii trip , but within 2 days this time the black paint had began to flake off . The Good , the Bad , and the Ugly : The Good : the WD TV Play Media Player does what it claims to do . It works to play Youtube , Netflix , Vudu video streaming . It ' s easy to set up . Also , it streamed Netflix smoother at the distance from my wireless router than my Wii did . If you look at the specs on this device and say " That is all I want in a media player " then the WD TV Play Media Player is for you . The Bad : the WD TV Play Media Player feels like a marketing partnership device more than a really handy tech gadget . It ' s lightweight , feels cheap , and is so upfront about making a paid streaming service easy to access that I can ' t help but think WD is hoping that Netflix , Vudu , and CinemaNow are going to start giving these away with membership contracts . There is no sense in which these acts like a device that will expand its offerings or capabilities even with a firmware update . The Ugly : the WD TV Play Media Player does exactly what it claims to do . It does not do more , it does not appear like it will expand . If it were advertised just as a " streaming media player " without the description lining out exactly what services it supports , it would be a huge disappointment . Instead , compared to either a networked BluRay or a Wii ( these I have , others may use PS3 / X - Box or TV with network capacity ) , it ' s just too limited . The Final Verdict : if you need a little box to play Netflix and not much else , this will do it . Otherwise , consider stepping up to a different gadget ( or even a multi - task gadget such as a network disc player ) that are more expensive , but more functional . You get what you pay for here , so it ' s not a bust . It ' s just not something to be overly excited about .

I bought an xPrintServer to print from my iPad to an HP Color LaserJet 2600n . I did my homework in advance and confirmed that my printer was supported by checking Lantronix ' s website - it was ( and still is ) listed as a supported printer . Bluntly : that was a lie . When the xPrintServer couldn ' t print a test page , I wrote to Lantronix tech support to ask for help and got this reply : & # 34 ; The 2600n is one of a handful of HP models that requires a proprietary for which HP cannot provide the source , since it was developed by a third party . The 2600n is the only one of that group for which we were able to reverse engineer the appropriate plug - in . However , in order to gain this support , you will need to update to the latest beta firmware . & # 34 ; I gritted my teeth and installed the beta firmware as instructed . Who cares as long as it works , right ? Well , it didn ' t work . Pages printed at half their regular size and the colors were washed out and almost invisible . I got another suggestion from tech support to switch to a black - and - white - only driver , but that didn ' t work at all . At that point , I gave up and returned the device to Amazon . On a positive note , tech support was great . I dealt with people who clearly know their product well and tried hard to help me . I appreciate that ! Unfortunately , I wasted a lot of time trying to get the setup going because their website incorrectly said that my printer would work with it . If it wasn ' t listed as supported and I bought it without researching first , then I would ' ve given the device 5 stars for build quality , ease of setup , and great tech support . I don ' t like that I was deliberately mislead into buying something known not to work , though .

I have been looking for a good way to control our HTPC from the couch and had thought that I finally found it . I first ordered the iPazzport " air mouse " with learning IR functionalityiPazzPort 2 . 4GHz Mini Wireless Fly Air Mouse Keyboard with IR Remote . This was a fairly well designed product . I really enjoyed the backlit keyboard and found it easy to type . Setup could not have been easier and the range was good . Unfortunately , I did not enjoy the air mouse functionality . I find it much more difficult to use than touch pads that I have tried in the past . It was a good product , just not the one for me . I returned the product to seller and had no problems at all . Because I enjoyed the keyboard aspect of it , I decided to try out a very similar product : theUnisen iPazzPort KP - 810 - 18R Wireless Keyboard and Touchpad with Learning Infrared ( IR ) Remote Control 2 . 4G RF Google TV CompatibleUnisen iPazzPort kp - 810 - 18R which is very similar to the first , but has a touch pad . I purchased the product from an Amazon seller called Key to success . I received my order very quickly and it was packaged well . So far so good ! The product was awesome ! In fact perfect ! Not only does the keyboard work well , the touch - pad is great , the buttons are well designed , setup is as easy as it can be , and the IR learning remote is fantastic . This seemed to be just the product that I was looking for . Then after a week everything went south . For no apparent reason the lazer pointer button jammed and would not turn off . I was able to dislodge it with a sewing needle and was still committed to it being a great product . Then the next day the IR remote randomly stopped working . I would press a button and I would get a quick blink of the LED but then nothing . Touching a button would not do anything unless I took out the battery . Ok so maybe we are down to a 4 star review now . I was still enjoying the rest of the product . Then the touch pad started working intermittently . Sometimes it worked great , and sometimes not at all . And then to top it all off , the battery stopped charging . How disappointing ! All of the reviews of this product were great . Maybe I just got a lemon . These things happen . So I decided to contact the seller of the device ( key to success ) to request a return or exchange . My first contact was four days ago , and I got no response . My second contact was yesterday , and still no response . I would like to give this product another try , despite my concern that it might not last very long . This has all the makings of a great product . It ' s well designed , looks good , feels good , works well . . . for a while . If I am able to get in touch with the seller and get a replacement I will write another review . This should be a five star product all day long , but after breaking within a week , it ' s lucky to get two stars .

Monster Gratitude In - Ear HeadphonesWhen I first received this box , I was extremely excited for the headphones . Overall , I would not recommend spending the money on these headphones . For the price , there are many other headphones out that are better in sound and provide better base and treble .
